title: The Betting Act,2025

sections.title: This Act may be cited as the Betting Levy Act, 2025, and Short title
sections.body:
shall come into operation on 1st January, 2026. and commen-
cement

sections.title: In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires— Interpretation
sections.body:
“betting” has the meaning assigned to the word in the Customs Cap. 322
and Excise Act;
“betting company” means a person licensed under the Betting Cap. 155
Control Act or any other written law to provide betting
services in the Republic;
“brick and mortar betting” means a betting activity conducted
at a physical premise where a customer places a bet in
person through cash, electronic means or any other payment
method;
“Commissioner-General” means the person appointed as
Commissioner-General under the Zambia Revenue Cap. 321
Authority Act;
Single copies of this Act may be obtained from the Government Printer,
P.O. Box 30136, 10101, Lusaka, Price K8.00 each
394 No. 27 of 2025] Betting Levy
“customer gaming account” means an account maintained
by a betting company for a customer in which deposits,
bets, winnings and withdrawals are recorded;
“deposits” means monies credited to a customer’s gaming
accountant for purposes of enabling that customer to place
a bet or participate in a betting activity, through cash,
electronic means or any other payments method, but does
not include winnings credited from previous bets placed
using funds already in the account;
“levy” means a betting levy charged under section 5;
“withdrawals” means monies paid out from a customer’s
gaming account, by a betting company, to the customer
through electronic means or any other payment method;
and
“Zambia Revenue Authority” means the Zambia Revenue
Cap. 321 Authority established under the Zambia Revenue Authority
Act.
Administration 3. This Act shall be administered by the Zambia Revenue
of Act Authority.
Powers of 4. The Commissioner-General shall, for the purposes of
Commissioner enforcing the provisions of this Act, have all the powers conferred
- General
on the Commissioner-General by the Zambia Revenue Authority
Cap. 321
Cap. 323 Act and the Income Tax Act.
Imposition of 5. (1) Subject to the provisions of this Act, a betting levy at
levy the rate of 5 percent shall be charged, levied, collected and paid by
a betting company.
(2) The levy shall be calculated on all deposits made to a
customer’s gaming account and on all withdrawals from a
customer’s gaming account.
(3) A betting company shall, within ten days after the end of
each month—
(a) submit a return of the levy collected to the Commissioner-
General in the manner determined by the Commissioner-
General; and
(b) remit the total levies collected for that month to the
Commissioner-General.
Betting Levy [No. 27 of 2025 395
(4) The Commissioner-General may appoint a person as an
agent to withhold a levy before facilitating any payments to, or
from, a betting company.
(5) A betting company that contravenes subsection (3) is
liable to pay to the Commissioner-General a penalty of three hundred
thousand penalty units for each month or part thereof during which
that contravention continues.
(6) The Commissioner-General may waive the whole, or part,
of the penalty due under subsection (5) of this section.

sections.title: (1) A betting company shall keep and maintain a record of Records of
sections.body:
the betting company’s operations for a period of six years. betting
operations
(2) A betting company shall maintain electronic records of all
deposits and withrawals, which shall include—
(a) a customer’s gaming account number;
(b) date and time of a transation;
(c) transaction amounts; and
(d) the levy calculated and deducted.
(3) A betting company shall ensure that the system used for
purpose of maintaing the electronic records referred to under
subsection (2) is capable of intergration with the systems used by
used by the Zambia Revenue Authority for automated reporting
and verification purposes.
(4) Despite subsection (1), the Commissioner-General may,
before the expiry of the period referred to under subsection (1),
by notice in writing, extend the period of keeping and maintaining
a record of operations by a betting company.
(5) A betting company that contravenes subsection (1) is liable
to pay the Commissioner-General a penalty of three hundred
thousand penalty units.
(6) The Commissioner-General may waive the whole, or part,
of the penalty due under subsection (3).
396 No. 27 of 2025] Betting Levy
Inspection of 8. (1) The Commissioner-General may, for the purposes of
betting
enforcing the provisions of this Act, at any reasonable time, without
operations
prior notice, enter any premises that the Commissioner-General
has reasonable grounds to believe is used contrary to the provisions
of this Act, and—
(a) inspect any records, article or document of a betting
company that has a bearing on an inspection;
(b) verify the returns submitted in accordance with section
5(3); or
(c) require a betting company to submit any other information
that the Commissioner-General may consider necessary.
Administrative 9. The Commissioner-General may issue administrative Rules
Rules relating to—
(a) the keeping of books of account for betting operations;
(b) the making of returns; and
(c) any other matter necessary for the administration of this
Act by the Commissioner-General.

sections.title: The Minister may, by statutory instrument, make
sections.body:
Regulations Regulations for the better carrying out of the provisions of this
Act.
